message:
refactor(core): remove asupersync-tokio-compat, add native hyper_bridge, harden safety

Replace the asupersync-tokio-compat shim with a native hyper_bridge
module in fcp-async-core that provides HyperIo and HyperExecutor
adapters directly, eliminating the intermediary crate dependency.

Key changes across core infrastructure:

- fcp-async-core: Remove AsupersyncIo/stdin()/stdout() re-exports,
  add hyper_bridge module with HyperIo<T> (pin-project-lite) and
  HyperExecutor backed by fcp_async_core::task::spawn. Add hyper 1.8.1
  and pin-project-lite 0.2 deps.

- fcp-host: Replace TokioIo/AsupersyncExecutor with HyperIo/HyperExecutor
  for TCP and Unix socket HTTP serving. Add wildcard "*" support for
  zone_allowed_connectors and zone_allowed_operations in enforcement
  policy ceiling checks, with tests.

- fcp-host supervisor: Guard against divide-by-zero in ResourceUsage
  utilization calculations when limits are zero.

- fcp-graphql: Migrate WebSocket test harness from tokio-tungstenite to
  native asupersync ServerWebSocket with manual HTTP header reading and
  WebSocketAcceptor. Remove asupersync-tokio-compat and tokio-tungstenite
  dev-deps.

- fcp-raptorq: InactivationDecoder::new() now returns Result with
  UnsupportedSourceBlockSize error for K > 56,403. Encoder validates
  source block size before encoding. Decoder uses local codec module
  instead of re-exported asupersync types.

- fcp-sandbox: Propagate setrlimit() errors on Linux and macOS instead
  of silently ignoring failures. Use i32::try_from() for landlock
  syscall fd values instead of bare `as i32` casts.

- fcp-ratelimit: Migrate leaky_spin_test from tokio to fcp_async_core
  runtime.

- fcp-sdk: Update migration module doc comment to remove tokio reference.

- Cargo.toml: Remove tokio-tungstenite from workspace dependencies.
  Add new connector workspace members.

- Cargo.lock: Updated to reflect dependency changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
